entries.raw_feedback_summary: The proposed versions looked too similar; they changed colors but not the underlying concept.
rule_updates: For creative or marketing pages, require structurally distinct directions.
Require a visual concept, motion concept, layout rhythm, and CTA logic for each version.

entries.raw_feedback_summary: The deployed A/B/C/D proposal slice looked much better after B/C/D became structurally distinct compositions: Cat Concierge, Studio Proof, and Demo Worlds, while A remained Guided Offer Map.
rule_updates: Preserve named directions as separate compositions, not token variants.
Use Better-like bold agency principles only as adapted inspiration: large type, simple pillars, proof surfaces, and confident CTA rhythm.
Pair playful mascot and motion with concrete demos, proof artifacts, mobile safety, reduced-motion fallback, and performance checks.
Treat PR, deploy, commit, screenshots, and test output as reusable evidence in future handoff packets.

entries.raw_feedback_summary: Future design directions should cross expressive themes such as lightness, freedom, novelty, distinctiveness, modern retro, art, futurism, pixel vibe, chill, calm, calculated minimalism, focus, dopamine color, artistic 3D/modeling, animation, written craft, and colorful visual language, then find SEO and usability compromises.
rule_updates: Use theme axes intentionally instead of selecting one generic style.
Pick a primary personality axis, a contrast axis, and a product-proof axis for each direction.
Keep core offer, headings, proof, and CTAs in static readable HTML.
Require performance, accessibility, mobile, reduced-motion, and indexability compromises before approving expressive visual directions.

entries.raw_feedback_summary: The current A/B/C/D GitHub preview is materially closer. The next step should refine polish, motion, proof, mobile conversion rhythm, and mascot usefulness without changing runtime architecture or adding unverified assets.
rule_updates: Treat accepted A/B/C/D directions as baseline and improve their clarity before inventing more variants.
For refinement slices, prefer sharper conversion hierarchy, useful proof artifacts, mobile-first rhythm, and purposeful motion over broad restyling.
Record public preview evidence and rollback path before claiming a design polish slice is ready.
Discard advisory model output when it focuses on workspace/process context instead of the redacted product brief.
